title: wangerinia minima
intro: Wangerinia minima is a species of flowering plant in the family Rutaceae, commonly known as the citrus family, and is native to the subtropical regions of South Africa. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating fever and digestive issues, due to its anthelmintic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Wangerinia minima include its ability to relieve symptoms of colds and flu, reduce inflammation, and support digestive health. Herbal preparations of Wangerinia minima can be found in various forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
